templates.template_description: California Bar-compliant demand letter format for personal injury cases. Includes California-specific requirements for statute of limitations (CCP § 335.1), demand amounts, and medical documentation.

rules.rule_name: California Statute of Limitations Section Required
rules.rule_description: California demand letters must reference statute of limitations (CCP § 335.1 - 2-year limit for personal injury)

rule_config.error_message: California demand letters must reference Statute of Limitations (CCP § 335.1)

rules.rule_description: Demand letter must state a specific total dollar amount

rule_config.error_message: Demand letter must state a specific dollar amount

rules.rule_description: Demand letter must include client's full legal name

rule_config.error_message: Demand letter must include client's full name

rules.rule_description: Demand letter must include the date of the incident

rule_config.error_message: Demand letter must include date of incident

rules.rule_description: California practice: detailed breakdown of medical specials (bills, treatment dates)

rule_config.error_message: California demand letters should include detailed medical specials breakdown

rules.rule_description: California practice: separate discussion of general damages (pain and suffering)

rule_config.error_message: California demand letters should discuss general damages separately

rules.rule_description: California letterhead should include State Bar number

rule_config.error_message: California attorney letterhead should include State Bar number

rules.rule_description: If demanding policy limits, must be clearly stated

rule_config.error_message: If claiming policy limits, must be explicitly stated

rules.rule_description: Demand letter must comply with California Insurance Code requirements for bad faith claims

rule_config.error_message: Consider including Insurance Code bad faith discussion if applicable

rules.rule_description: California comparative fault percentages must be disclosed (Civ. Code § 1431.2)

rule_config.error_message: Consider disclosing client's comparative fault percentage if known

rules.rule_description: Future medical expenses should be calculated using life care planner methodology

rule_config.error_message: Consider including future medical expense calculation if significant

rules.rule_description: Include loss of consortium claim for spouse if applicable

rule_config.error_message: Consider including loss of consortium claim if client is married

rules.rule_description: If punitive damages sought, must establish malice/oppression/fraud (Civ. Code § 3294)

rule_config.error_message: Must establish clear basis for punitive damages under Civ. Code § 3294

rules.rule_description: Specify response deadline (typically 30 days for California)

rule_config.error_message: Demand letter must specify response deadline (typically 30 days)

rules.rule_description: Notice that mediation may be required before litigation

rule_config.error_message: Consider noting mediation requirement under California law

templates.template_description: California Superior Court complaint format following California Code of Civil Procedure

rules.rule_description: Complaint must identify all parties with addresses

rules.rule_description: California complaints typically include general allegations section

rule_config.error_message: California complaints should include general allegations section

rules.rule_description: Each cause of action must be separately stated (CCP § 425.10)

rule_config.error_message: Each cause of action must be separately stated (CCP § 425.10)

rules.rule_description: Complaint must include prayer for relief with specific damages

rule_config.error_message: Complaint must include prayer for relief (CCP § 425.10)

rules.rule_description: If jury trial demanded, must be clearly stated

rules.rule_description: Complaint should anticipate demurrer response timeframe

rule_config.error_message: Consider including anticipated demurrer response timeframe

rules.rule_description: Must include ADR notice as required by California law

templates.template_description: Motion for summary judgment under California Code of Civil Procedure § 437c

rules.rule_description: Notice must follow California format requirements
